text: We need to find and then add a keyboard shortcut (and command) for the #gui to collapse parent - in doing so, the caret should jump to the parent of the current task whose children are now collapsed
Kalamu #hub — one always-on local server, all projects in a sidebar (SPEC.md 'Hub' section is canonical)
Hub sidebar is render-once: project names/open-task badges only refresh on navigation — consider a hub-global SSE stream for live cross-project badge updates #hub #gui

Show a `Copy [Mod] [C]` button on each node when it is focused. On hover, show the same button - but without the kbd part since the keyboard shortcut will only copy the node where the cursor is at that point in time
A launchd-managed hub keeps running old server code after a CLI update (assets refresh, process doesn't) — consider the hub noticing a newer bundle on disk and exiting so KeepAlive restarts it #hub
Design + write real landing content (hero, live outline demo island, install, agent story) #landing
Deploy landing to kalamu.dev (CF Pages or Vercel, root dir packages/landing) + DNS at registrar #landing

Split/merge round-trip loses the boundary space when the caret sits after it ("Parent |Split"): commit-time whitespace normalization trims the before-half's trailing space, so a later Backspace-merge yields "ParentSplit". Exact round-trip would need the split commit to skip trailing-space normalization - core commit/tokens change, needs a decision.
Tag-chip colour popover inherits pre-wrap whitespace from the outline's .text, so its sections get ~22px line-box gaps (pre-existing; sidebar popover unaffected). Fix: white-space normal on .popover
Mod+Shift+3/4/5 are macOS screenshot shortcuts — the hub never sees them, so projects 3–5 aren't reachable by keyboard on a default Mac. Keep as-is, remap, or add a second binding?
UpdateChip and HubHint are independent sticky footers; when both are visible they stack as two bars. Consider merging into one footer row or prioritising one. #ui
Guard init/hub registration against non-repo directories
Decision: fresh interactive init asks 'This doesn't look like a code repository — initialise Kalamu in <cwd> anyway? [y/N]' when cwd has no .git, .gitignore, or package.json (no walk-up); default no
Guard lives on init and open's bootstrap only — hub registration stays a never-prompting side effect of use
Non-TTY runs (agents, pre-git-init scaffolds) and re-init never prompt; open's bootstrap asks one repo-aware question, never two
Implemented 2026-07-13: looksLikeRepo in cli/src/context.ts, wiring in cli/src/index.ts, SPEC init+open sections updated

Logo decided (bronze fanned-k). Mark = a tiny outline (bullet on the stem, checkbox nested beneath) that reads as a k; extended into the 'kalamu' wordmark for the app header, mark-alone cream-on-bronze tile as the favicon. Shipped: Wordmark.svelte, --brand token in app.css, favicon in index.html, assets/logo.svg + assets/icon.svg.

Per-project installed-app (PWA) icons: serve a dynamic /manifest.webmanifest under the hub (name = project name, start_url/scope = /p/<slug>/) with icons rendered in the project's colour (sharp is available in the toolchain to rasterize the tile SVG server-side, matching the dynamic favicon). Today the manifest + PNG icons are static bronze — good enough that installs no longer show a browser-generated letter.
